nts1.d: Berlin techno aficionado Prosumer lays it out for all the wannabes. A harmonious lecture in house and soul, Prosumer’s here to share.

Settle in for weekly shows from Margeaux aka @marg.mp3, soothing with an hour of selections, offering an eclectic range of sounds old and new.
From the host: _"some of isa's favorite songs from artists taking part in no music for genocide !
go to nomusicforgenocide.org to learn more + join the movement < 3"_
LA native Bianca Lexis showcases a monthly, genre-blending hour, exploring the different facets of pop, rock, synth and house music. all in panoramic view, for you.
Sno presents an hour of music from Ghanaian highlife and afrobeat artist, Ebo Taylor, who sadly passed away last month.
An hour of music from the sweet and soulful selector by way of Brooklyn, NY.

Rising alongside the sophisti-pop strain of British New Wave, sophisti-funk names an early-to-mid-1980s current in R&B and funk that fused post-disco rhythms with high-gloss studio modernism. Defined by digital precision, immaculate surfaces, and a newly cosmopolitan sensibility in Black popular music, it recast funk’s language of grit and excess into one of polish, restraint, and spacious minimalism — total sonic luxury.
Forged in major-label studios with state-of-the-art equipment, with its machine-forged rhythms and melodic extravagance, sophisti-funk laid the sonic and aesthetic foundations for much followed: from street soul and new jack swing through the ’90s boy-band explosion into vaporwave and contemporary digital R&B."
This Guide To presents a selection of tracks that incapsulates the sound – featuring production work from Kashif, Jam and Lewis, Nick Martinelli and more, as well as artists like Luther Vandross, The System, Patrice Rushen, Reid and Babyface among others.
Outsider sounds with one of the station OGs.
2 hour bi-monthly excursions into sound and dissonance. Music label with John T. Gast. Regular guest selections from friends and affiliates.

A regular hour from Philly-based digital shoegazers, Full Body 2 - made up of Cassidy Rose Hammond, Dylan Vaisey, & Jack Chaffer.
From the host: "Our best friends Vera and Carrie (aka Vertigoaway and Purity://Filter) have graciously stepped in for us this month while we're on tour across North America. They've blessed us with 40 minutes of heavy hitting dance music and a glorious android ringtone review segment to close it out. thx love uu
An hour where anything goes, leaning toward the hazy and the off-kilter, courtesy of Melbourne's Bridget Small.

nts1.n: POST PUNK BRITAIN: IN FOCUS - THROBBING GRISTLE (R)
nts1.d: No artists resonate more with the experimentation and nebulous genre-lessness of the UK post punk era than Throbbing Gristle. This two hour episode dives deep into their work, featuring related projects, solo work and more.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ SPIRIT BLUE
The NTS Breakfast Show w/ DARE BALOGUN
SOUP TO NUTS W/ SHY ONE
Steven Julien Presents: The Apron Show

nts2.d: Contemporary British AV artist, collagist, and NTS host Mark Leckey takes to the airwaves to play oddities from his collection. Everything from doo-wop to gabber.

North London native and Hyperdub signee Loraine James is an electronic producer who effortlessly blends Electronica and IDM with whispers of jazz and ambience all wrapped up in the artists key avant-garde aesthetic and sensibility.

Loops, remixes, edits and reinterpretations from DJ and programmer miss sheitana, oscillating between post-club, rap, baltimore club, bass, ambient and remixes from the limbos of the internet.
From the host: _"just trying to make you listen to anything
° layers of GTA ° perplex piano ° dissonant singeli ° miner's strike ° enhanced pretty boy swag ° old but gold °
pain music +++++++
anybody want a drink before the war ?"_

London musician kwes. returns to music with his first new record in 8 years. “Kinds” is a new project exploring ambient, drone, and modern classical sounds inspired by the tetherless creative spirit of children. He shares some of the new tracks with NTS, alongside indie rock, Ethiopian jazz or more.

Optimo is JG Wilkes & JD Twitch; record label, and longstanding Glaswegian party. You can expect a lot from their new monthly slot, sure to dig up some obscurities from post-punk, new wave, EBM, house and techno. Bags of unreleased stuff, too.

nts2.n: THE LUNACY OF FLOWERS W/ Jonas Karsten
nts2.d:
Dave I.D takes us through industrial, and techno – melodic and dark. New and classic goth, his selection sets him apart from most, branching decades and scenes.
From the host: "Helsinki-born, Berlin-based producer and artist working across experimental electronics, ambient, metal and other extreme sounds. Guest hour for Hedges David (The Lunacy Of Flowers)"

nts2.n: Cosmic Soundz w/ Salvatore: International Funk
nts2.d:
Lima, Peru vinyl obsessive Salvatore Dellepiane shares selections from his treasure trove of rare and obscure boogie and funk from all across the world.
From the host: "A selection of funk, disco, and soul from across the globe, bringing together records from the US, Peru, Chile, Panama, Nigeria, Ghana, Italy, Brazil, and beyond. The mix moves through different rhythms and styles but stays rooted in groove, focusing mostly on obscure and lesser-known records."

Los Hitters is the collective partnership of Joe Cristando (Couple Two T’ree) and Rene Lopez (Suavecito Souldies). A collaboration that links the two from Brooklyn, NY to Oakland, CA. Their mixes focus on soul sounds comprised of their vinyl collection.

nts2.n: Fusion Batches w/ DJ Morse Code & XL Middleton
nts2.d:
DJ / Producer Morse Code brings us a monthly all vinyl mix of jazz-funk, jazz fusion, and rare groove in the tradition of his Fusion Batches mixes from the mid 00's, which championed dollar bin heat as well as the rares, all in the name of the hunt for the best grooves!
From the host: "this episode features an exclusive guest mix from Pasadena's own XL MIddleton.  In addition to being a stellar and versatile modern funk musician, he is also a purveyor of incredible music from all over the world and owns Salt Box Records in LA.  He blessed us with an all Japanese jazz-funk mix!"

DJ Kita is a Los Angeles based DJ most known for her energetic sets and dynamic hip hop selections. Her sets are fun and unpredictable, and capture the youthful essence of hip hop culture. Kita is a resident DJ and co curator of LA’s premiere black and queer underground party called Hood Rave. She has toured both nationally and internationally.

nts2.n: The Last Playlist w/ Luis Felipe Farfán & Maustetytöt
nts2.d:
"The Last Playlist" explores mortality via songs people want at their funerals. Guests share stories ranging from hilarious to heartbreaking, discussing their musical choices while exploring death, grief, and what makes life worth living. Each episode weaves together music and conversation to celebrate life's complexities and honor its inevitable end.
From the host: "In this special episode, we sit down with Maustetytöt to explore the intersection of Finnish identity and art. From the complexities of working as sisters to the songs they want played at their funerals."

Veteran NTS host Hampus brings you Doing Time: a genre-agnostic monthly trip through quality records from around the world’s past and present, featuring regular appearances from musical friends and family.

nts2.n: OKONKOLE Y TROMPA - PAM B2B MINIATURE (R)
THE BADDIS SOUND W/ DANNIELLA DEE
Martyn's Darkest Light - Mainstream Records special
THE WINDMILLS OF YOUR MIND w/ Taylor Rowley
SEA TO SEA w/ Diana Ratsamee

subtle.n: Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

subtle.d: With a monthly show on Subtle, Niceup is a London based DJ with a unique style. Pushing UKG, Breakbeat, Bass, Electro and 140. Weaving through genres showcasing new releases and older cuts.

Jedah started DJ'ing at the tender age of 18 years of age specialising in UK Garage/Grime. 

Jedah is currently the DJ for Grime Original Hollowman Jendor of the former Grime Collective Essentials/ OG's.

Responsible for “that massive grime tune with the Psycho strings”, UK producers/DJs Trends & Boylan are well versed in what the scene has to offer. They use this platform to showcase music from across the 140 spectrum from Grime to Dubstep. Anything goes!!

XOSÉ is a DJ and aspiring producer mixing and making mostly UK Garage, Grime and UK Funky. FORWARD PRESSURE largely focuses on the aforementioned UK born genres, though you can expect a variety of UK influenced sounds.

Sempra is a producer & DJ based in London, specialising in the fusion of UK Garage, Jungle and Breakbeat with dreamy vocal melodies and the beautiful instrumentation found in Soul and contemporary RnB. Sempra’s consistent output has been released by labels such as Future Classic and Headroom, gaining industry support from the likes of Nia Archives, Sherelle, Yung Singh and Dr Dubplate.

Cicely is a northern raised London based music photographer and DJ. From learning to mix during lockdown, she has since played at clubs across the country in venues such as Fabric, Corsica and Fold.

Cicely runs a collective and events night under the name NRG CRU which focus heavily on the high energy rave sound. She is highly influenced by the northern sound she grew up with, and you can hear this within her sets. Expect heavy blends of techno and hardcore while she shines the light on her favourite northern artists.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Bristol-born and London-based, Not Bad For A Girl resident DaiSu has been involved in the music scene since 2018 and has featured on stations such as Radio 1 Dance, BBC 6 Music and Melodic Distraction, as well as playing on line ups for Keep Hush, Skiddle and Red Bull. Her penchant for bass, breaks and 2-step has landed her gigs supporting acts such as Interplanetary Criminal, Main Phase, Craigie Knowes and Big Ang. In September 2022, her debut EP ‘Lightwork’ was released with Gather Records and was featured in DJ Mag's staff picks that same month.

Vinyl DJ from Leigh-on-Sea. Loves collecting records with a wide array of taste ranging from House and Techno to Grime, Dubstep and Jungle. Garage holds a special place in his heart and makes up the largest part of my collection and DJ sets.

DJ & Producer hailing from Watford influenced by Hip-Hop, Dubstep, Grime with releases on NSX records & Ghost Phone, spinning electronic genres from R&B edits, Drum and Bass and everything in between.
Highlander brings you 2 hours of the freshest Jungle, Hardcore, Dubstep and Breaks. 3rd Saturday 7-9pm lock in!

2Step Selects is a monthly show hosted by the Choki Biki Records crew. Founded in Dublin, Ireland, CBR consists of DJ Sharpson, 2step.png, Frame Shift, and MICK. The show consists of an all round breaks, electro, ukg and jungle hour, followed by an Irish showcase, demonstrating all the best Ireland has to offer collective/party/Dj & Producer wise!

Saraphim (aka RNS & RDubz) are two Essex based Producer/DJ’s currently focused on Dubstep & the 140 spectrum, mixing ethnical and grime influences into their music. You can expect anything from deep and melodic meditative vibes to high energy driven music.
London based DJ who enjoys exploring the darker, bassier corners of Jungle, Breaks, Techno and Drum & Bass, especially when it is laced with a bit of acid.

subtle.n: The Ice Box Sessions with Frigid

dublab.n: Echo Park Film Center - Optical Track
rerun: Shared Frequencies presents: Blossoms of Iran w/ nohup

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Digital Drums OR NO ONE TURNED AWAY
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.

Victor Rodriguez and Chris Bowen - Bears in Space
Elyn Kazarian – Her Vision Her Voice
